note: Manual layer of dashboard/portfolio-hq.html: everything here comes from live PostHog reads, founder statements, or weekly readouts — not derivable from status.json. Update this file (and bump asOf), then run ./agentic-os refresh — it regenerates portfolio-hq.html as its last step.

founderCommand.title: S0 — 20-minute device test that reorders everything below it
founderCommand.body: WP-47 merged (#104, 18b8764): the sign-in wall is now instrumented and 1.1.1 (25) is ready. But instrumentation tells you the drop happens, not whether Sign in with Apple is BROKEN. The 1.0.9 read showed 5 non-cancel ASAuthorizationError 1000 failures and 0 sign_in_completed. If SIWA is genuinely failing for new users, that is a production outage and S2 (value-before-account) would be treating a symptom. This is the cheapest test in the portfolio and it changes the order of everything after it. Founder-only: needs a physical device and the live App Store build.
founderCommand.prompt: RunSmart iOS S0: on a physical device, install the LIVE App Store build (1.1.0) using an Apple ID that has never signed into RunSmart. Attempt Sign in with Apple. Record: does it complete, or does it fail with ASAuthorizationError 1000? Screenshot the result. This distinguishes a production auth outage from a persuasion problem and determines whether S2 is worth building.

founderCommand.title: Ship RunSmart 1.1.1 (25) — wall instrumentation is merged and waiting
founderCommand.body: PR #104 merged to main as 18b8764. Full suite 313 passed / 0 failed, xcresult-verified. Version bumped 1.1.0 (24) -> 1.1.1 (25) in the project only — no archive, no ASC upload, no submission was performed. Until this ships, the wall events exist only in the repo and the activation cliff stays unmeasured in production. Best done after S0, so a confirmed SIWA fix can ride the same build.
founderCommand.prompt: RunSmart iOS: archive 1.1.1 (25) from main (18b8764), export with ExportOptionsAppStoreUpload.plist, upload to App Store Connect, create the 1.1.1 version, attach the build and submit for review. Verify the archived Info.plist before uploading. Report the upload timestamp.

founderCommand.title: Resumely: cohort is NOT mature — do not read it until ~2026-08-18
founderCommand.body: WP-48 merged (#112, 47f1476) and the answer is a clean negative: 0 of the required 20 clean uploaders, 9 hours after release. Clean arrival is 4.7 file-selectors/week, which projects to 2026-08-18 for a readable cohort. It also found 2 blocking measurement defects — most importantly the 12.5% baseline is NOT reproducible on 1.4.3, because resume_upload_succeeded sits behind the sign-in guard and is unreachable for guests. Reading early would have declared a win that was pure denominator substitution. Fix the denominator before the window opens, not after.
founderCommand.prompt: Resumely iOS: implement the measurement fixes from docs/qa/reports/wp48-post-1.4.3-cohort-read-2026-07-20.md before the cohort matures. Redesignate the activation denominator to a pre-auth event (resume_file_selected) and recompute the baseline on that same definition so the win rule is reproducible on 1.4.3. Then scope S2 instrumentation (score_screen_signin_tapped, file-picker outcome events, job_source) for 1.4.4. Do not read the cohort — it is not mature until ~2026-08-18.

founderCommand.title: Resumely Web PR #117 is BLOCKED — merge order would regress a working feature
founderCommand.body: WP-49 is good work but was held, not merged. The WP-39-style guard covers the ats-check INSERT only; the three READ paths (auth/callback, convert-session GET and POST) request the six new columns with no fallback. The migration is not applied to production, so merging and deploying first makes those selects return 42703, user_id/converted_at never get set, and session conversion — which works today — silently stops. Fix is to add the same fallback to the three selects, which also makes deploy order irrelevant. Full reasoning is in the PR review comment.
founderCommand.prompt: ResumeBuilder Web PR #117: apply the isUndefinedColumnError fallback to the three read paths that currently request the new anonymous_ats_scores columns without a guard — src/app/[locale]/auth/callback/route.ts and both SCORE_COLUMNS selects in src/app/api/public/convert-session/route.ts. On undefined-column, retry with the original narrow column list and skip materialization. Add a regression test proving conversion still sets user_id/converted_at when the new columns are absent. Also add a Privacy Policy line covering short-term anonymous resume retention (EN + HE). Do NOT apply the migration.

tracks.name: Portfolio Activation Playbook v2
tracks.pill: CURRENT — BOTH NEW BUILDS IN ASC REVIEW
tracks.standing: The canonical activation contract remains Intent → Setup → First value → Verified value → Repeat value. Founder-excluded mature D7 snapshots still drive decisions. As of 2026-07-19: RunSmart 1.1.0 and Resumely 1.4.3 are both Waiting for Review on ASC; public store still serves prior live FTUX builds (1.0.9 / 1.4.2) until Apple approves. Neither product has a mature clean post-release cohort yet. Existing 2026-07-12 D7 snapshots remain the last decision-grade read; 2026-07-19 PostHog refresh is directional only.

tracks.name: RunSmart FTUX upgrade
tracks.standing: Founder update 2026-07-19: RunSmart 1.1.0 is in ASC Waiting for Review. Public App Store still serves 1.0.9 (23) until Apple approves. Read-only PostHog verification froze a 2026-07-16 05:30 UTC snapshot on build 23: 2 clean physical App Store installs, 0 mature D7 entrants, and both public entrants emitted non-cancel sign_in_failed with no sign_in_completed. This remains a P0 signal to verify on the public binary. E1 is BLOCKED on missing exposure/assignment instrumentation and absent lifecycle evidence.

If sign-in succeeds, capture S6 empty-goal and S1 plan-generation failure/retry evidence in the same physical-device session
Founder decision: defer E1 until sign-in works and at least 10 clean mature-D7 entrants exist; do not build assignment instrumentation now

Keep share_progress_tapped; share_progress_completed stays parked until sharing is an active growth lever
PR #96 is open with two pushed documentation commits; merge after review

tracks.name: Resumely FTUX upgrade
tracks.standing: Founder update 2026-07-19: Resumely 1.4.3 was just sent to ASC review. Release A 1.4.2 (12) remains the public live binary until Apple approves. A separate Claude Code session owns Stories 7 and 8 toward combined 1.5.0. The 1.4.2 readiness audit found 82 exact-version events across 5 raw people, 4 excluded, and 0 clean post-release entrants. Measurement-ready is NO: mature D7 is 0/0 undefined, not 0%.

tracks.name: Resumely web export — observability fix
tracks.standing: The web export-observability fix merged in PR #114 on 2026-07-11. Core CI, GitGuardian, CodeRabbit and Vercel checks passed; the known Cloudflare Workers check failed independently of the Vercel production path. The export mechanism was already QA-verified end to end. The next valid read is after five real founder/QA-excluded web completers pass through the newly instrumented export path.

items.text: Optimization page now emits optimized_viewed, export_cta_seen, export_pdf_tapped; download route emits authenticated export_started then exactly one of export_success/export_failed, all tagged with optimization_id, platform=web, source, format, renderer/error code

Regression test failed 3/3 before the fix, passes 3/3 after; targeted + full ESLint clean (11 pre-existing warnings, 0 new errors); production build succeeds; TypeScript clean outside documented pre-existing test errors; smoke test generated a 7,328-byte PDF
PR #114 merged 2026-07-11; Vercel check passed and the instrumented export path is now available for measurement
Re-measure optimized_viewed → export_cta_seen → export_pdf_tapped → export_started → export_success/export_failed after 5 real web completers

artifacts.note: Durable artifacts built across Builder OS, Agentic OS and the product repos, so nothing gets lost. `state` is the honest storage truth, not a quality rating: `safe` = committed and pushed to a branch on origin; `branch-only` = pushed but never merged to main, so it is invisible to anyone reading main; `local-only` = exists on one disk and one `git clean` from gone. Verified against origin on 2026-07-15 — re-verify before trusting a link after any branch cleanup.

suggestedSessions.model: Manual founder watch - App Store Connect
suggestedSessions.title: Watch ASC review for RunSmart 1.1.0 and Resumely 1.4.3
suggestedSessions.why: Both binaries are Waiting for Review. The portfolio state flips the moment either goes Ready for Sale — measurement windows and public verification targets change with them.
suggestedSessions.outcome: ASC status noted; when either ships, Portfolio HQ asOf + post-live clocks updated same day
suggestedSessions.prompt: Open App Store Connect for RunSmart and Resumely. Confirm 1.1.0 and 1.4.3 are Waiting for Review (or note the new state). If either is Ready for Sale, update portfolio-hq-manual.json with exact version/build, UTC go-live time, and start a clean post-live measurement note; then run ./agentic-os refresh.

suggestedSessions.model: Manual founder action - public App Store build on a physical device
suggestedSessions.title: Disprove or reproduce the RunSmart sign-in P0
suggestedSessions.why: Both clean public build-23 entrants emitted non-cancel sign-in failures and neither progressed. One founder-device attempt can distinguish a likely configuration failure from a tiny-sample signal before six more days of cohort time pass.
suggestedSessions.outcome: Either confirm sign-in works on public 1.0.9 (23), or capture the failure message + authorize Supabase inspection
suggestedSessions.prompt: Install public RunSmart 1.0.9 (23), attempt Sign in with Apple once, record UTC bounds and visible result, then flush and inspect the matching PostHog event. If it fails, inspect provisioning/ASC capability and authorize the Supabase connector for provider-config inspection before changing code.

suggestedSessions.model: Claude Code - continue only after the current Stories 7-8 session integrates
suggestedSessions.title: Execute WP-46: combined Release B+C continuation
suggestedSessions.why: The founder chose one 1.5.0 target while keeping Stories 7 and 8 under their current owner. WP-46 turns the remaining work into sequential, evidence-gated stories without interfering with the live app.
suggestedSessions.outcome: Stories 9-13 implemented toward a 1.5.0 release-readiness handoff; no App Store/archive action
suggestedSessions.prompt: Execute WP-46. Reconcile and preserve the current Stories 7-8 work, verify the approved Story 9 additive backend contract, then implement Stories 9-13 sequentially. Produce a 1.5.0 release-readiness handoff and stop before any archive or App Store action.

suggestedSessions.title: Land both production-readiness reports
suggestedSessions.why: RunSmart PR #96 and the Resumely 1.4.2 report branch contain the current measurement contracts and prevent the dashboard from falling back to stale review-era narratives.
suggestedSessions.outcome: Both readiness reports reviewed/merged with uncertainty language preserved (no 0/0 → 0%)
suggestedSessions.prompt: Review and merge RunSmart PR #96 and open/review the Resumely measurement-report PR from codex/release-a-measurement-baseline-2026-07-16. Preserve their uncertainty language and do not convert 0/0 into 0%.

suggestedSessions.title: WP-29 S5 — anonymous session carryover after signup
suggestedSessions.why: S1-S4 funnel fixes shipped; the first authenticated dashboard still risks looking empty without carryover, which kills activation right after the conversion moment.
suggestedSessions.outcome: Guest optimization/job context survives signup so the first dashboard is not empty
suggestedSessions.prompt: In the ResumeBuilder web repo, implement WP-29 S5: anonymous session carryover after signup so the first dashboard is not empty. Read tasks/MEMORY.md and the WP-29 notes first. Deliver one work packet only: design + implement carryover for guest resume/job/optimization context into the authenticated session, with acceptance criteria and checks. Do not touch Stripe, Premium CTAs, or Gate A.

suggestedSessions.title: X/Twitter trend + user-language pass (filed 2026-07-19)
suggestedSessions.why: Completed: language clusters + activation/GTM hooks + do-not-claim list. Native X still blocked (login wall); next scrape needs founder X session.
suggestedSessions.outcome: Brief filed at executive-os/research/2026-07-19-x-twitter-activation-gtm-hooks.md — founder picks copy lines next
suggestedSessions.prompt: Search X/Twitter and the open web for current job-hunt / resume / ATS pain and beginner-running coach pain. Cluster objections and exact phrases people use. Produce: (1) Resumely activation + GTM hooks, (2) RunSmart activation + GTM hooks, (3) what NOT to claim. Keep it decision-ready, not a novel. Prefer evidence quotes over vibes.

growth.note: WP-32 closed inconclusive on 2026-07-12. X/Twitter → activation & GTM research filed 2026-07-19: executive-os/research/2026-07-19-x-twitter-activation-gtm-hooks.md (native X login-walled; hooks from HU/ATS/AI-resume language + prior demand-mining). Next: founder picks 1 Resumely + 1 RunSmart ASO/first-screen line to draft; no publish without approval. WP-31 remains the next approved draft-only organic asset.

WP-32 FB groups closed inconclusive — 3 reactions, 1 comment, ASC lift unavailable
Michal — set up on Codex, build Michal's OS (parked pending her Gemini→OpenAI migration, decided 2026-07-09)

kk: SIGN-IN (2026-07-19, hardened): RunSmart has 7 non-cancel ASAuthorizationError code-1000 failures across 3 distinct fresh installs — iPhone99,7 and iPhone12,1 on public 1.0.9 (2026-07-15) and iPhone17,2 on build 24 (2026-07-19) — with 0 sign_in_completed on any of them. The single sign_in_completed on 1.0.9 (2026-07-16) is the founder's already-provisioned iPhone 13 (iPhone14,5). Supabase auth logs covering today's 11:03-11:04 UTC failures contain no /token request, so the failure occurs on-device before Supabase is contacted; Supabase provider_disabled is ruled out for that window (older 07-15 failures are outside the 24h log retention and cannot be checked). Code 1000 is also produced by a device with no iCloud account and by locally-installed builds whose provisioning lacks the SIWA capability — build 24 was not an App Store install, so that data point is weak. Verdict: strong unresolved P0 signal, still NOT a proven production outage. One clean repro on an iCloud-signed device with the public binary settles it.

CLEAN FUNNELS (2026-07-19, founder/QA/bot-excluded, 90d): RunSmart 23 organic App Store users → 1 onboarding_started → 1 plan_generated → 0 run_started (95.7% lost at the first screen). Resumely 145 reached → 20 tapped upload → 16 uploaded → 8 job_added → 2 optimization_started → 1 export_success (87.5% of uploaders never optimize).

CONTAMINATION SCALE: Resumely raw dashboards are ~90% non-organic — 45 is_internal_tester persons (1,216 events) plus automated QA chains firing 3-9 events/sec (latest run 2026-07-18) generate nearly all raw fit_check_*, save_failed and optimization_apply_* volume. RunSmart has no is_internal_tester flag at all; exclusions there rely on behavioural heuristics (>=6 sessions or >=3 app versions) plus 39 simulator persons on model x86_64.

CORRECTION: free_ats_completed DOES carry score_bucket (an earlier read queried the wrong property names and wrongly reported it null). Live 90d: 5 events '0-40' and 5 events '41-60' — every real free-ATS user saw a score of 60 or below, half saw 40 or below.

BUILD CONTENT: RunSmart 1.1.0 (24) in review contains NONE of the activation fixes (Adaptive Coach Phase 1 only). Resumely 1.4.3 (13) in review PARTIALLY ships the top fix — tappable 'Sign in to Optimize' under the guest score card plus diagnosis persistence across sign-in; the CTA tap remains uninstrumented and sign-in does not auto-continue into optimization.

ASC 2026-07-19 (founder-confirmed): RunSmart 1.1.0 is Waiting for Review; Resumely 1.4.3 was just submitted for review. Public store still serves RunSmart 1.0.9 (23) and Resumely 1.4.2 (12) until Apple approves.

PostHog refresh 2026-07-19: RunSmart directional ordered funnel 63→6→3→0→0 (Jun 1–Jul 19); Resumely directional ordered funnel 14→4→2 upload→optimize→export (last 30d). Decision-grade mature D7 remains 0/13 and 0/73 from 2026-07-12.
Resumely PR #97 reconciled Release A Stories 1-6 and the exact 1.4.2 (12) version stamp onto origin/main; 151 tests passed, 1 intentional skip, 0 failures, so main can rebuild the active binary.

Resumely 1.4.2 production readiness is No: 82 exact-version rows across 5 raw people, 4 excluded and 0 clean post-release entrants. Mature D7 is 0/0 undefined; tagging is complete and the live property audit found no prohibited content.

RunSmart 1.0.9 post-live snapshot at 2026-07-16 05:30 UTC found 2 clean physical installs, 0 mature entrants and 5 non-cancel sign-in failures across both people with 0 sign-in completions. This is a P0 signal, not a proven outage at n=2.
Both remain at 0% real D7 activation on the 2026-07-12 mature read: Resumely 0/73; RunSmart 0/13.

Resumely D7 wall = first seen→upload→optimization: 73 first seen, 9 uploaded, 0 optimized, 0 exported. PR #114 web export observability is merged; wait for 5 real web completers before reading that new path.
RunSmart wall = install→onboarding and plan→run: 13 mature installs, 1 onboarded/planned, 0 ran. WP-40 S1+S2 merged via PR #84; WP-42 has no clean production disclosure cohort yet.
Traffic collapsed post-launch-push; WP-29 web funnel fixed + verified.
One backend tester account owned 22/23 of all Resumely Supabase optimizations pre-hardening — analytics identity/alias wiring shipped 07-07 (Story 1) to fix this going forward.

Garmin: brand attribution + Internal-Test-vs-Commercial tiering are well-grounded in Garmin's public docs (matches 3 real rejections). Fee structure for RunSmart specifically and the Gate 1-4 checklist are founder-confirmed directly with Marc via email/attachments (2026-07-08) — the 07-06 research pass's "unconfirmed" flag was based on Garmin's generic public docs, not RunSmart's actual terms with Marc; no further written confirmation needed.
ku: When will ASC clear RunSmart 1.1.0 and Resumely 1.4.3, and do the public binaries change the sign-in / export walls?
Can the next Hebrew distribution test produce a measurable answer after WP-32 closed without attribution? It must use unique campaign links and verified analytics access before publishing.
Did Resumely Stories 2-4 (PR #90, merged 07-08) actually move the upload-picker friction numbers for real (non-founder) traffic? Re-read once enough post-merge volume exists.
Does the merged web export-observability path reveal real web export failures after 5 real completers, or does it confirm the button works for organic users?

Can a founder-device public RunSmart 1.0.9 sign-in succeed? This is the fastest discriminator for the n=2 P0 signal before inspecting provisioning, ASC capability and Supabase Apple provider configuration.
Is Resumely's cumulative clean export count 0 or 1? The saved clean July 6 report says 0 while a later manual dashboard claim says 1; resolve with a reproducible query before citing it.
Voice-coach flag: DECIDED 2026-07-09 — deferred (stays off), review at the 07-12 activation re-read or after WP-40 lands. No longer an open question.
uk: Michal Track C fully drafted — blocked only on her live Gemini account.

WP files lag Codex/Cursor sessions — dashboards lie until packets are updated. Three Codex/Cursor sessions found work sitting uncommitted or on detached HEAD this week (ResumeBuilder iOS APP x2, RunSmart Web) 07-06/07/08 — branched, verified, and preserved each time (PR #89, #90, #116), but a recurring risk worth remembering before trusting 'no local changes.' WP-32/33/34 were the exception this round — Cursor updated and pushed the packets itself (commit fe9ad95).

status.json's needsDecision still lists "RunSmart iOS: if build 8 is rejected again" — stale, from before 1.0.7(21) shipped and passed review. Not a portfolio-hq bug; RunSmart iOS's own task files need a refresh to clear it. Same staleness pattern flagged by ./agentic-os verify for COO-LATEST-REVIEW.md (references build 18, current is build 21).
ResumeBuilder IOS APP is missing tasks/ERRORS.md and docs/agent-os/project-context.md — flagged as a required-read gap by two separate Codex sessions now.

Artifacts strand silently. Both FTUX audits — the evidence base for all 13 Resumely stories and all three RunSmart work packets — sit on branches never merged to main, and the Resumely one was untracked in every branch until 2026-07-15 (rescued via PR #96). The Artifacts tab now lists each one with its real storage state; treat `branch-only` as one branch-cleanup away from invisible.
RunSmart E1 is deliberately deferred until sign-in works and at least 10 clean mature-D7 entrants exist; assignment instrumentation is not active work.
RunSmart iOS has ~30 untracked Finder-duplicate '* 2.swift'/'* 2.md' files cluttering the repo — pre-existing, same issue already named in status.json's blockers as blocking clean archives.
uu: Does the PostHog leak run both ways (Resumely → RunSmart project)?
Do other dashboards carry founder/QA contamination (the 50% inflation pattern)?
Is 'activation' the right north star before there's traffic to activate?

weekPriorities.title: RunSmart — close the sign-in question before writing any more RunSmart code
weekPriorities.body: Evidence hardened 2026-07-19: 7 non-cancel ASAuthorizationError 1000 across 3 fresh installs (1.0.9 public and 1.1.0 build 24), 0 successes on those devices, and the ONLY sign_in_completed is the founder's already-signed-in iPhone 13. Supabase auth logs for today's 11:03-11:04 UTC failures show no /token request at all, so the failure is on-device (Apple side) before Supabase — provider_disabled is ruled out for today. The 2026-07-19 device test is BLOCKED (never reached SignInView). Do one clean repro: wipe the app, confirm the device is signed into iCloud, install PUBLIC 1.0.9 from the App Store, tap Sign in with Apple once, record UTC + exact on-screen text. If it fails on a clean iCloud-signed device, this is a production outage and outranks every other item in the portfolio. If it succeeds, the wall is a persuasion problem and item 3 takes over.

weekPriorities.title: Resumely — ship S2 telemetry while 1.4.3 sits in review
weekPriorities.body: 1.4.3 already carries the top cliff fix (tappable 'Sign in to Optimize' + diagnosis persistence). Half a day of work makes the resulting read attributable: score_screen_signin_tapped, file-picker outcome events (selected/cancelled/error + file type/size), and a job_source (url vs paste) property on free_ats_completed and optimization_started. Ships in 1.4.4/1.5.0. Highest lift-per-hour in the portfolio because Resumely is primary and its window closes 2026-08-01.

weekPriorities.title: RunSmart — S1 wall instrumentation in 1.1.1 (carry the error code)
weekPriorities.body: Add sign_in_wall_viewed, sign_in_tapped, sign_in_wall_abandoned (once per session; background/terminate after >=5s with no attempt), real screen names, and attach error_domain/error_code to every wall event. This makes item 1 self-answering for every future user instead of needing a device repro. Session replay only after privacy guardrails (consent/opt-out, input masking, retention, access control) — ship events without replay rather than delay. Also fixes the plan_generation_failed/succeeded double-fire.

weekPriorities.title: ASC — wait honestly on both binaries
weekPriorities.body: Both are Waiting for Review; public store still serves RunSmart 1.0.9 (23) and Resumely 1.4.2 (12). Do not resubmit or claim live. When either goes Ready for Sale, record exact version/build + UTC and start a clean post-live window. Predeclared decision rules: Resumely = >=20 clean post-1.4.3 uploaders, win at >=30% optimization_started (baseline 12.5%); RunSmart = >=30 clean installs on the de-walled build, win at >=20% onboarding_started (baseline 4.3%). Below those denominators the read is inconclusive, not a verdict.

weekPriorities.title: Do not spend on acquisition, and do not chase Adaptive Coach Phase 2 yet
weekPriorities.body: Both funnels destroy installs before value, so paid traffic compounds the loss. Adaptive Coach Phase 2's gate (2 weeks live AND >=20 real adaptive_coach_shown users) is arithmetically unfillable at ~25 installs/month with ~96% lost at the wall — roughly 1 real user/month reaches Today. Phase 2 unblocks only after items 1 and 3 move the entry funnel.
